title: DUE PROCESS VIOLATION CATALOG
subtitle: Every Procedural Due Process Violation by McNeill

violations.violation: Ex parte order without required statutory findings
violations.authority: MCL 722.27a(3) — must find clear and convincing evidence of danger

violations.violation: Denial of right to be heard before deprivation
violations.authority: Mathews v Eldridge 424 US 319 (1976) — balancing test
violations.remedy: De novo hearing before new judge

violations.violation: Denial of right to present evidence
violations.remedy: New hearing with full evidentiary presentation

violations.violation: Denial of right to cross-examine witnesses
violations.remedy: New hearing with cross-examination rights preserved
Disqualification + vacatur of all biased orders

violations.violation: Complete deprivation of parental liberty without adequate process
violations.authority: Troxel v Granville 530 US 57 (2000) — fundamental right

violations.violation: Failure to make required findings of fact
violations.remedy: Remand with instructions to make findings

violations.authority: Santosky v Kramer 455 US 745 (1982) — clear and convincing standard

violations.remedy: New hearing with adequate preparation time
